assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map (impact-map.md) contains a 'Flagged Ambiguities' section listing 5 explicit ambiguities: (1) 'Search should be faster' -- no quantified performance targets, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' -- no relevance criteria defined, (3) 'Add filters' -- no filter types specified, (4) 'Should be fast enough' -- non-functional requirement completely unquantified, (5) 'Don't break existing functionality' -- no regression test baseline specified. All 5 clearly exceed the minimum of 3.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map contains a 'Scope Exclusions' section stating: '"Better UI" (Non-MVP): This requirement cannot be planned. No design mockups are provided, and no frontend repository is in scope (only trustify-backend is available). This item is excluded from the implementation plan entirely. It should be planned separately once a frontend repository and design specifications are available.' No task files address UI changes.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain the required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify/Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements. For example, task-1-define-search-filter-model.md has ## Repository, ## Target Branch, ## Description, ## Files to Create, ## Files to Modify, ## Implementation Notes, ## Acceptance Criteria, ## Test Requirements.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks contain explicitly labeled '**Assumption pending clarification**' statements. Examples: Task 1 Description: 'Assumption pending clarification: The specific filter fields (entity type, date range, severity) are assumed...'; Task 2 Description: 'Assumption pending clarification: "Results should be more relevant" is interpreted as adding ts_rank-based full-text search scoring...'; Task 3: 'Assumption pending clarification: The filter parameters are added as optional query parameters...'; Task 4: 'Assumption pending clarification: "Search should be faster" is interpreted as adding HTTP-level caching...'; Task 5: 'Assumption pending clarification: The feature description says "don't break existing functionality" but does not specify a regression test baseline...'. Assumptions also appear in Acceptance Criteria sections.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files have '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend' as a single repository name. No task lists multiple repositories. Verified in: task-1 (line 1-2), task-2 (line 1-2), task-3 (line 1-2), task-4 (line 1-2), task-5 (line 1-2).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain all four required sections: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with detailed content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified across task-1 through task-5.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Cross-referencing against repo-backend.md: Task 1 creates modules/search/src/model/mod.rs (new file under existing modules/search/src/ directory) and modifies modules/search/src/lib.rs (exists in repo). Task 2 modifies modules/search/src/service/mod.rs and common/src/db/query.rs (both in repo). Task 3 modifies modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (in repo). Task 4 modifies modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (in repo). Task 5 modifies tests/api/search.rs (in repo). All paths are present in or plausible extensions of the repo-backend.md structure.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/model/paginated.rs. Task 2: references modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/error.rs. Task 3: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs, modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs. Task 4: references modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs. Task 5: references tests/api/search.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs, tests/api/advisory.rs. All are specific file paths from repo-backend.md.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 (line 4-5), task-2 (line 4-5), task-3 (line 4-5), task-4 (line 4-5), task-5 (line 4-5).

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks contain valid digest markers: Task 1: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:3b3ceceb47a34470d700f4f86186dbec82690744383fd7987cc987f6367debef' (64 hex chars). Task 2: 'sha256-md:e06d157e5c134d9c41c123f160082b90b18a04708e7be5338eb87443f36a3401' (64 hex chars). Task 3: 'sha256-md:a33e1160ac3230a1dd95d6b064b486a4cf4829b4bc4ae6e3c7fe2417a53940f1' (64 hex chars). Task 4: 'sha256-md:50b7eaa606bfe699825c4c08359b03224c28d14607511435763a382f429dd32d' (64 hex chars). Task 5: 'sha256-md:9c7a793c90afb96e63cbbe802b2d91cc69e7029e5ab6bf7e4ea0d1005dbdcea6' (64 hex chars). All use sha256-md: prefix with exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All tasks include conventions with prescribed-format rationales. Task 1: 'Applies: task creates modules/search/src/model/mod.rs matching the convention's Rust module scope.' and 'Applies: task creates modules/search/src/model/mod.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' Task 2: 'Applies: task modifies common/src/db/query.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' and 'Applies: convention has no file-type restriction (broadly applicable).' (for Framework convention). Task 3: Three conventions all with 'Applies: task modifies modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' Task 4: Two conventions with correct format. Task 5: Two conventions with correct format. No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. All rationales follow the prescribed format naming specific files and scopes.
